message:
fix(storage): auto-repair missing analytics tables when schema version markers lie

Historical canonical rebuild paths could produce databases whose
_schema_migrations version markers claim the current schema while
post-V10 analytics tables (daily_stats, embedding_jobs, token_usage,
token_daily_stats, model_pricing, message_metrics, usage_hourly,
usage_daily, usage_models_daily, sources) were never materialized.

Add repair_missing_current_schema_objects() called on every
FrankenStorage::open() after migrations. It probes each required
table with a lightweight SELECT...LIMIT 1 and, if any are missing
despite the version marker being current, idempotently re-runs
MIGRATION_FRESH_SCHEMA to backfill the DDL.

- REQUIRED_CURRENT_SCHEMA_TABLE_PROBES: const table of 10
  (name, probe_sql) pairs for the required analytics tables
- error_indicates_missing_table(): pattern-match on "no such table"
  in error messages to distinguish missing-table from other errors
- Test: drops 9 analytics tables, forces version marker to current,
  verifies open() recreates all of them

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
